title: Xanthocephalum humile
taxonomy: Xanthocephalum humile is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a member of the tribe Senecioneae, a group of plants that also includes the genera Senecio and Packera. Within the tribe, Xanthocephalum humile is part of the subtribe Tussilagininae. This classification is based on the plant's morphological characteristics, such as its leaf shape and flower structure. The classification helps to identify Xanthocephalum humile as a distinct species within the Asteraceae family.
uses: Xanthocephalum humile is traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help in treating various health conditions. The plant is also used to treat digestive issues and skin problems due to its antibacterial properties. In addition, Xanthocephalum humile is thought to have antispasmodic effects, which may help in relieving muscle cramps and spasms. More research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential health benefits.

intro: Xanthocephalum humile, also known as yerba de pasmo or yerba de los pobres, is a perennial plant native to the Americas, belonging to the family Asteraceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant effects, making it beneficial for treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Xanthocephalum humile include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are commonly used to treat respiratory issues and as a natural expectorant.
